meta.game: World of Warcraft Classic: Mists of Pandaria
meta.purpose: Craft target list plus supporting sub-recipes for pricing and profession planning.
meta.reagents_source_note: Reagents were filled from accessible recipe/database mirrors and wiki sources when direct Wowhead access was unavailable.

stocking_rules.flask: medium to high volume if margins hold
stocking_rules.potion: medium to high volume if margins hold
stocking_rules.alchemy_transmutation: keep this conservative and favor Living Steel, Trillium Bar, and the strongest gem markets
stocking_rules.alchemy_mount: very low volume, only when margin comfortably covers the heavy vendor reagent spend
stocking_rules.inscription_card: very opportunistic, only if flip value still meaningfully beats materials and repost friction
stocking_rules.tailoring_epic: craft only if margin is clearly high and competition is low
stocking_rules.food: medium volume for the strongest stat foods; keep cache-recipe foods lighter until margins stabilize
stocking_rules.feast: low to medium volume, with more focus around raid reset and guild raid nights

potion.note: Model Potion Master as 20% expected extra output per potion craft for valuation. Adjust this multiplier if your own craft logs suggest a different long-run average.

rules.expected_common_pigment_per_mill_note: Inference used for planning: common pigments are described as yielding 2-4 per mill, so the planner uses the midpoint when valuing herb -> pigment -> ink paths.
rules.common_pigment_yield_note: Milling typically yields common pigments, with uncommon pigments appearing as bonus outcomes when applicable.
rules.pandaria_note: Any common Pandaria herb can produce Shadow Pigment, while Misty Pigment is the uncommon Pandaria result used for Starlight Ink.

Shadow Pigment.note: Treat Misty Pigment as an expected-value rebate when milling Pandaria herbs for Shadow Pigment: about 1 per 20 herbs for regular herbs and about 2 per 20 herbs for Fool's Cap.

Midnight Ink.note: Legacy ink trade priced through Ink of Dreams.

Lion's Ink.note: Legacy ink trade priced through Ink of Dreams.

Jadefire Ink.note: Legacy ink trade priced through Ink of Dreams.

Celestial Ink.note: Legacy ink trade priced through Ink of Dreams.

Ethereal Ink.note: Legacy ink trade priced through Ink of Dreams.

Ink of the Sea.note: Legacy ink trade priced through Ink of Dreams.

Starlight Ink.note: Pandaria rare ink trade priced through Ink of Dreams.

Blackfallow Ink.note: Legacy ink trade priced through Ink of Dreams.

inscription.vendor_exchange_note: Pandaria ink traders also let scribes trade Ink of Dreams into older inks when direct milling is less attractive.

Dreamcloth.note: Planner compares the volatile Dreamcloth variants and will use Dream of Destruction too if Chaos Orb is priced in non_ah_reagent_prices or the snapshot.

crafted_from_options.name: Dream of Destruction
crafted_from_options.note: Included for completeness; this path will be ignored unless Chaos Orb is priced locally.

Imperial Silk.note: Imperial Silk can come from the daily Silkworm Cocoon route or from Song of Harmony. Planner compares both crafted paths and keeps direct AH Imperial Silk disabled because force_crafted_cost_items models your own tailoring cooldown usage.

crafted_from_options.name: Silkworm Cocoon
crafted_from_options.note: Daily cooldown path that turns 8 Bolts of Windwool Cloth into one Silkworm Cocoon, which yields Imperial Silk.

tailoring_phase4_catalog.scope_note: Tailoring recipes available by MoP Classic Phase 4, centered on AH-facing crafts and core subcrafts for this planner, with the rank 3 tailor-only perks included so the phase-4 catalog is not missing the profession's personal enchants.
tailoring_phase4_catalog.source_note: Recipe names, reagent counts, and unlock notes were checked against Warcraft Wiki/Wowpedia tailoring recipe pages, especially the Pandaria tailoring patterns table plus individual item or pattern pages when the aggregate table snippet was ambiguous.

excluded_after_phase_4.family: Celestial Cloth and its 553 tailoring epics

groups.note: These do not participate in AH planning, but they are available by Phase 4 and were added here so the tailoring catalog is not missing the profession-only rank 3 recipes.
